The patient has had diabetes for one year without complications. According to established guidelines, diabetics should begin annual screenings for diabetic retinopathy either upon diagnosis or shortly afterward, depending on whether they have type 1 or type 2 diabetes. For both types of diabetes, early detection through regular eye exams helps manage potential vision impairment effectively.
\boxed{A} Based on the guideline stating that individuals with diabetes should undergo yearly screening beginning soon after their diagnosis, the correct option is \boxed{A}.
